title: hieracium caesium subsp. pseudobasifolium
intro: Hieracium caesium subsp. pseudobasifolium is a lesser-known sub-species of the Hieracium caesium plant, commonly known as the mountain hawk's-beard, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. It has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and possessing antimicrobial properties. Hieracium caesium subsp. pseudobasifolium can be used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
